Title: DISTINCT
Description: IfSELECT DISTINCTis specified, only unique rows will remain in a query result. Thus, only a single row will remain out of all the sets of fully matching rows in the result.
You can specify the list of columns that must have unique values:SELECT DISTINCT ON (column1, column2,...). If the columns are not specified, all of them are taken into consideration.
ClickHouse supports using theDISTINCTandORDER BYclauses for different columns in one query. TheDISTINCTclause is executed before theORDER BYclause.
Selecting data with the different sorting direction:
Take this implementation specificity into account when programming queries.

DISTINCTworks withNULLas ifNULLwere a specific value, andNULL==NULL. In other words, in theDISTINCTresults, different combinations withNULLoccur only once. It differs fromNULLprocessing in most other contexts.
